note: group_support_revisions is a Group submodule that adds per-group revision permissions by decorating the default relation permission provider. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded); no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 Group site, which submodule makes revision access over grouped entities depend on the group, and does it have a settings form?
evals.expected_output: group_support_revisions; it has no config/UI/settings — enabling it is the whole setup. It adds per-group revision permissions.

evals.prompt: How does group_support_revisions add its revision permissions to every grouped entity type without editing each relation plugin?
evals.expected_output: It registers a decorator (SupportRevisionsPermissionProvider) that decorates the group.relation_handler.permission_provider service, so revision permissions are added across the whole permission-provider chain.

evals.prompt: For which grouped entity types does group_support_revisions actually expose revision permissions on a Drupal 11 site?
evals.expected_output: Only relation plugins whose target entity type implements RevisionableInterface (and that already define entity permissions); non-revisionable types get none.

evals.prompt: Which four per-group revision operations does group_support_revisions add for a revisionable grouped entity?
evals.expected_output: view all revisions, view specific revisions, revert revision, and delete revision (named like `{view all|view|revert|delete} {plugin_id} entity revisions`).

evals.prompt: If I enable both gnode and group_support_revisions on a Drupal 11 Group site, what do I gain for group nodes?
evals.expected_output: Per-group revision permissions (view all/view/revert/delete revisions) are automatically added for the revisionable group_node relations, letting group roles control node revision access.
